strategy: This is a 16,190-health Darkmoor finale, so run it as a team: a hitter, supports who trap and blade, and a healer who avoids healing him into a punish. He resists Death 67%, so hit with Life (+30%).
Use Mass Feint (+75%), not single Feint. A single Feint triggers Dark Tribute, which clears your traps and blades him; Mass Feint does not trigger it.
Keep chip damage on Kain to control his interrupt cycle. Any attack pauses the cycle a round and resets it to the start, so steady hits stall the Star dispel and Monster Mash.

Do not heal, Drain him, or cast harmful Charms on him: heals trigger an interrupt Vampire (even pet may-cast heals), Drains make him Aegis-blade, and harmful Charms Disarm the caster. Avoid sun spells too, since they give him a protected universal dispel.
Set up traps with Mass Feint and blades on the hitter, keep resetting his cycle with attacks, then land one big Life hit.
cheatNotes: A single Feint on Kain triggers Dark Tribute: he clears your universal traps and blades himself +30%. Use Mass Feint (+75%) instead, which does not trigger this.
Any harmful Charm on Kain gets the caster Disarmed. Do not weaken or curse him directly.
A Drain spell makes him Aegis-protect a +100% Balanceblade on himself. Do not drain him.
Any heal triggers an interrupt Vampire that drains a wizard and heals the caster. Avoid healing during the fight.
Pet may-cast heals also trigger the Vampire. Bench heal-casting pets.
The heal cheat does not fire for a beguiled wizard.
A spell with a sun in it (Power Nova and the like) makes him protected-universal-dispel himself. Avoid sun spells.
His interrupt cycle begins on round 2 and repeats every 3 rounds until reset.
Cycle round 1: a Star Supercharge gives him a +10%-per-pip universal damage charm and a free pip.
Cycle round 2: a Star spell lands a same-school dispel on every wizard, so your next attack of that school fizzles.
Cycle round 3: Monster Mash on a random wizard.
Attacking Kain pauses his cycle for a round and resets it to the beginning. Keep chip damage on him to stall the dispel and Monster Mash.
Damage-over-time spells only reset his cycle on the round you cast them, not on each tick.
